text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from massive amounts of data, including books, articles, and websites. This training data allows the model to predict the next word in a sentence, building responses step by step. Users guide the model through prompt engineering, crafting clear instructions to get useful outputs. LLMs have impressive capabilities, such as writing essays or answering questions. However, they also have limits: they can produce inaccurate information, lack true understanding, and may reflect biases in their training data.
